title: Dual Mandate vs. Price Stability First
objective: Explain the difference between a dual mandate and a price-stability-first mandate, and understand why both positions are held by credible Tier 1 institutions.

sources.title: Federal Reserve — What economic goals does the Fed seek to achieve?
sources.trust_rationale: US central bank FAQ — primary authority on the Fed's own statutory mandate.

sources.title: European Central Bank — Monetary policy introduction
sources.trust_rationale: Eurozone central bank — primary authority on the ECB's Treaty-defined mandate.

beats.narration: Every central bank is given a mandate — a legal instruction about what it must achieve. But not all mandates are the same, and the differences lead to genuinely different policy choices in identical situations.

visual.svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 480 160' width='480' height='160'><rect width='480' height='160' fill='#f8f7ff' rx='10'/><rect x='30' y='50' width='180' height='60' rx='8' fill='#e0e7ff' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='120' y='82' text-anchor='middle' font-size='13' font-weight='700' fill='#4338ca'>Federal Reserve</text><text x='120' y='99' text-anchor='middle' font-size='11' fill='#4338ca'>Dual Mandate</text><rect x='270' y='50' width='180' height='60' rx='8' fill='#e0e7ff' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='360' y='82' text-anchor='middle' font-size='13' font-weight='700' fill='#4338ca'>ECB</text><text x='360' y='99' text-anchor='middle' font-size='11' fill='#4338ca'>Price Stability First</text><text x='240' y='35' text-anchor='middle' font-size='12' fill='#6b7280'>Two central banks, two mandate designs</text></svg>
visual.alt: Two boxes side by side: Federal Reserve with a Dual Mandate and the ECB with Price Stability First

beats.narration_intro: Here is where two of the world's most credible central banks genuinely disagree.
beats.question: Should a central bank be required to target employment as well as price stability?

positions.label: Dual mandate (Fed approach)
positions.narration: The Federal Reserve operates under a dual mandate: maximum employment and stable prices are co-equal goals, set by law. When the two goals conflict, the Fed must weigh both — it cannot sacrifice employment simply to hit an inflation target.

positions.label: Price stability first (ECB approach)
positions.narration: The European Central Bank's Treaty mandate puts price stability first, always. Employment is only a secondary concern, activated once price stability is secured. The ECB argues that stable prices are themselves the best long-run foundation for jobs.
